blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It seems like there's no one here.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: It's better. Whether it is mara or angel, I honestly don't have the confidence to fight them.
Canis tilted his head.
He didn't expect such a weak wor to come from Harvest, who had inherited Arcane's memories.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You're different from Master.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Memories alone do not determine personality. I was designed to protect you. To be blunt, without Ikael's help, we won't be able to complete our mission.
It came down to that.
The key to returning to their original world lay with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Still, without disabling the Meca System, there won't be any opportunities at all. Should we take a look around first?
Canis started walking while surveying his surroundings.
Suddenly, he felt something odd and approached the wall.
The panels were lined up at regular intervals, resembling books placed on a library shelf.
When he touched a panel, he felt a faint vibration.
The moment he pressed it down gently, there was a click sound.
Fearing an alarm might go off, he quickly stepped back.
humming
A humming sound was heard as the panel slid out.
Canis grabbed the panel and pulled it out completely.
A transparent rectangular circuit board, like glass, was connected to the panel.
He examined it both vertically and horizontally.
When he touched the panel with his thumb, letters made of light were engraved on the glass plate.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I see. This is the Angel's Archive.
Canis realized, recalling the enormous amount of panels embedded in Ingris.
Ingris was a space filled with knowledge.
As he touched the flashing arrow on the panel, the page turned.
As he admired it, the pages continued endlessly with text.
It was a high-density information system that was incomparable to books.
Unable to read the text, his gaze naturally fell to the numbers written at the bottom of the glass plate.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The first thing that came to mind was the note his master left behind.
This was all that Arcane had left behind. It was probably a serial number identifying the panel's location.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis was certain that his master had been to Ingris, and that he had realized something there and discovered a way to enhance dark magic.
Canis examined the text on the glass plate.
He couldn't even guess how to pronounce it.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Harvest, do you know what this says?

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: No. It's the first time I've seen this language too. We might need Arin.
At that moment, the letters began to collapse and then reappeared in the language they spoke.
Automatic language conversion? That's ridiculous.
Ingris wasn't a space created for Canis.
Therefore, it could be assumed that the panels recorded every kind of language that existed in the world.
It was an incredible accumulation of information.
Canis looked at the letters engraved on the glass plate again.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What was written on the page was information about a dream.
Skimming through it, he began to read aloud.
…Dreamo is a world one can enter through dreams. The method is to find the Dreamer Louver in the dream, but since he is in a place unrelated to events, it takes concentration to find him.
Usually, he is cooking or taking care of children in a house.
Since only Louver has color, it is not difficult to distinguish him.
He turned to the next page.
When you meet him, hand him some money and say you are looking for work.
He will offer a few dream jobs, and if you refuse three times, he will send you to Dreamo.
If you find a job you like, you can enjoy the dream as long as you want, but you won't remember it when you wake up.
Canis pursed his lips and looked at Harvest.
As Harvest shrugged his shoulders, Canis turned back to the panel and continued reading.
In the world of Dreamo, there are countless objects that ignore the law of causality because time does not exist.
To bring an object into reality, five conditions must be met.
First, help the owner of the object and receive it as a reward.
Second, go to the government office and report the object as stolen...

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: That won't do. It's prohibited by the law.
Canis's voice abruptly cut off.
As he slowly turned his head, there was a pretty woman standing at the first corner of Ingris.
Her skin was light purple, her eyes were sharply slanted.
She had lush curly light green hair, with a red triangle floating on top of her head.
It was Unihorn Mara.
Canis and Harvest, filled with tension, stared at her without moving.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Please, could you slowly put that back where it belongs? Even an archangel is not allowed to browse Ingris without the librarian's permission.
Canis put the panel back in its original place without taking his eyes off her.
The panel slid back in and became indistinguishable from the others.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I'm sorry. This is my first time here.
Canis, who decided to buy some time to think, started by apologizing.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: I thought so. If you were a true citizen, you wouldn't break the law.
Anyway, it's been a while since a heretic came here.
Canis realized that the shape of her mouth and her pronunciation were in sync.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: She wasn't communicating telepathically.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: How do you know our language?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Huhu, I'm a language collector. Plus, as the librarian of Ingris, wouldn't it be strange if I couldn't communicate?
Actually, in terms of number of times, it's quite a lot.
I'm talking about people who speak the same language as you.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Do you know a person named Kadum?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Aha! So that's it? Of course, I know. About 200 years ago, in your time, heretics came here. They asked to extend the lifespan of a man named Kadum by 300 years.
Canis listened to her and was convinced.
Ingris was undoubtedly a place that controlled human lifespan.
But how?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It couldn't be explained solely by the unimaginable density of information.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Is that even possible? How do you change a person's lifespan?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Ha, I get asked this question a lot. I don't know why people come here without knowing.
The librarian walked around with her hands clasped behind her back, like a teacher.
Her eyes were filled with affection as she looked at the panel.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: How can we change lifespan? Because this place is connected to the Akashic Records.
Canis also knew about the Akashic Records.
It was a word he often encountered while studying mythology.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: A supernatural collection of information that is said to contain all records of the universe.
The fact that the same word was used in Heaven meant that the myth was true.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It's impossible. What you're saying is a lie.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Hoh? Why?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: There is no such thing as a record containing everything in the world. How would you verify it's complete? It would inevitably be exaggerated to some extent.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Hmm, humans are indeed human. Well, at your level of thinking, that might be correct.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis felt a bit indignant.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Then you're saying it's not true?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Akashic Records aren't something that grand or magnificent. This world itself is the Akashic Records.
The librarian pointed at the panels.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Ingris uses Meca System to access Akashic Records. By twisting the equations, it changes the truth. This is called hacking. Adjusting a human's lifespan is a simple task.
Canis felt a sense of crisis.
The technological power to destroy the truth with the power of Meca System was at a level that could never be achieved unless humanity's paradigm changed drastically.
The manager snapped his fingers to get attention.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Enough with the explanations. If you're here to make a deal, let's head over there. You can change your lifespan at the central control device.
She gave him a sly look and pointed to the machine at the center of the crossroads.
Canis was reminded of the black market dealers he'd seen in Radum.
Suddenly, the situation felt surreal.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You want to make a deal? When you probably know what the situation is outside right now?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Hmm? Outside? Is something happening in heaven?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You don't know?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Of course, I don't know. I'm the librarian of Ingris. It's overwhelming just processing the information here. I haven't been outside for thousands of years.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis had a feeling that what she said was true, except for the part where she said it as if she was too busy to care.
She was isolated here.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If a librarian with the power to alter the Akashic Records had access to the world's information, who knows what catastrophic events might occur.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I thought it was a bit odd that a mere first-rank Mara was managing this place. Well, is it rather a good thing for me?
In any case, avoiding a conflict with her was impossible.
Even if he just made the deal and left, she wouldn't just stand by and watch him deactivate the security system of Zebul.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You said deal, what kind of deal are you talking about?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: Oh, really. You know everything yet being like thios? I mean lifespan, lifespan. I can extend anyone's lifespan.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: And what price must I pay?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: The librarian held her thumb and index finger slightly apart and winked.

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: It's very cheap. Just one word.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: A word? You want me to pay with a word?

blocks.speaker: Unihorn Mara
blocks.content: As I said before, I'm a language collector. That's why I'm smart. That's why I'm also the librarian of Ingris. Any word is fine. Just sell me one word you know.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If I sell you a word, I'll forget that word forever, won't I?
